note: DB-side daily_notes were unreachable this run (database locked / mid-write). Themes block is built from the last 5 podcast scripts in podcasts/, plus standing listener priorities recorded in dossier listener_focus fields.
summary: Listener (Rockville Democrat, MD-8 / D19 / Council D7, first-time primary voter) is in the middle of the candidate-deep-dive cycle. The single-candidate series and Andrew Friedson deep-dive are the lead deliverables. Standing priorities remain MCPS school funding mechanics, Wootton High School relocation, sanctuary-policy clarity across the leading Democratic candidates, and fiscal restraint on property tax.
themes: Single-candidate deep-dive cadence | Listener prefers one candidate per episode over multi-candidate news roundups; the May 18-25 gap-fill incident reinforced this preference
Wootton High School Crown relocation | Recurring listener-flagged topic; appears in dossiers for Friedson, the executive race, and the MCPS school-board context

Sanctuary policy across leading Democrats | Listener wants to know who actually opposes sanctuary protections; trust act + county values act voting record is consistent across Friedson / Glass / Jawando
Property tax discipline | Listener tracks property-tax votes by line; Friedson's 4-for-4 no-vote record on Elrich proposals is the cleanest signal
MCPS rating system | Listener wants to know how Rockville schools score and how the new rating is calculated — a state/MCPS topic, not a county-executive lever
avoid_for_next_episodes: Listener-frustration-narrated-back-to-listener framing — has shipped across at least five episodes
